summary: Violations issued to legally operating (licensed) waste haulers nearly quadrupled in fiscal 2023 to 2,008, then fell to 1,163 the next year. BIC's own reporting says the fiscal 2023 surge came from clearing a pandemic-era violations backlog, made larger by the agency's expanded traffic-safety enforcement authority starting in fiscal 2022, and that a new Violation Unit created in fiscal 2023 worked through that backlog, driving the fiscal 2024 decline. The fiscal 2025 uptick is attributed to more joint enforcement operations with the NYPD and New York State Department of Transportation.
